title: GCP Monitoring integration setup and troubleshooting
content: To set up GCP Monitoring, you need to create a Service Account in your GCP project with the 'Monitoring Viewer' and 'Compute Viewer' roles. Generate a JSON key for this service account and upload it to CloudDash. If metrics are not appearing: 1. Ensure the Google Cloud Monitoring API is enabled in your GCP project. 2. Verify the JSON key is valid and not expired. 3. Check if the GCP project ID matches exactly. Common error 'PermissionDenied' means the service account lacks the required viewer roles. This section provides additional context to ensure comprehensive documentation. The CloudDash platform is built with robustness and scalability in mind. When encountering issues, always verify your configuration settings and ensure that your network allows communication with our endpoints. If you continue to experience difficulties, please consult the troubleshooting steps outlined previously. Our support team is available to assist you with any advanced configurations or unexpected behavior. Remember to keep your agent versions up to date to benefit from the latest features and security patches. Furthermore, monitoring your usage limits can prevent unexpected service interruptions. For detailed API specifications, refer to our developer portal. We are committed to providing reliable service and continuous improvements to our platform. Should you have any feedback or feature requests, do not hesitate to reach out. Thank you for choosing CloudDash as your monitoring solution. We hope this guide has been helpful in resolving your query.
